docs.title: WeaveRx
docs.text:
Medical AI GitHub issue triage with auditable drafts, local safeguards, and human-in-the-loop defaults.

WeaveRx classifies MONAI, nnU-Net, and related project issues — reproducibility blockers, dataset access, subgroup performance, privacy/DICOM, and clinical validation requests — and produces a review-ready draft with sources and safeguard scores (local heuristics, no extra LLM calls).

docs.title: Quick links
docs.text:
• README on GitHub — install, screenshots, quickstart
  • Configuration — environment variables
  • Examples — JSON samples and CLI captures
  • Ethics — not for clinical use; human review required

Install: pip install weaverx

docs.title: See it in action
docs.text:
Command: weaverx triage --repo Project-MONAI/MONAI --issue 42 --mock

Safeguard checks are advisory — they flag drafts for review; they never auto-block posting.

Text fallbacks: examples/captures/

docs.title: GitHub Action
docs.text:
Dry-run triage on new issues via action.yml:

- uses: FratresMedAI/WeaveRx@v0.1.3
  with:
    repo: ${{ github.repository }}
    issue_number: ${{ github.event.issue.number }}
    dry_run: "true"
  env:
    XAI_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.XAI_API_KEY }}

docs.title: Documentation map
docs.text:
Topic Page Medical AI categories reference/categories.md Draft safeguards reference/safeguards.md CLI flags and JSON reference/cli.md LLM providers reference/llm-providers.md Releasing releasing.md

Local preview: pip install -e ".[docs]" && mkdocs serve — see Documentation setup.

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text: WeaveRx reads settings from environment variables and CLI flags. CLI flags take precedence where both apply.

docs.title: GitHub
docs.text: Variable Required Description GITHUB_TOKEN For writes Personal access token or GITHUB_TOKEN in Actions. Needed to post comments or apply labels. Optional for read-only triage on public repos.

docs.title: LLM providers
docs.text:
WeaveRx uses LiteLLM. Set one provider's API key.

Variable Provider Description XAI_API_KEY Grok (default) xAI API key ANTHROPIC_API_KEY Anthropic Anthropic API key OPENAI_API_KEY OpenAI-compatible OpenAI, Azure OpenAI, vLLM, or other OpenAI-compatible gateways OPENAI_API_BASE OpenAI-compatible Custom API base URL (optional; omit for api.openai.com) WEAVERX_LLM_PROVIDER All Override default provider: grok, anthropic, or openai WEAVERX_LLM_MODEL All Override model id (e.g. anthropic/claude-3-5-haiku-20241022)

CLI equivalent: --llm-provider grok|anthropic|openai

docs.title: Offline and mock modes
docs.text:
Variable Default Description WEAVERX_MOCK off Set to 1, true, or yes for fully offline mock GitHub + mock LLM

CLI equivalents: --mock (offline GitHub + mock LLM), --mock-llm (real GitHub, mock LLM)

docs.title: Safeguards
docs.text:
Local heuristic checks on generated drafts. No LLM calls on this path.

Variable Default Description WEAVERX_SAFEGUARDS 1 Set to 0, false, or no to disable WEAVERX_SAFEGUARD_ENTROPY_MAX 5.5 Shannon entropy threshold (bits per character) WEAVERX_SAFEGUARD_MAX_CHARS 6000 Maximum draft length before flagging

CLI equivalents: --safeguards / --no-safeguards

docs.title: GitHub Action
docs.text: The composite action passes inputs as environment variables. See action.yml and GitHub Action in this site.

docs.title: Quick reference
docs.text:
# Offline demo — no env vars
weaverx triage --repo Project-MONAI/MONAI --issue 42 --mock

# Real LLM, dry-run
export XAI_API_KEY=xai-...
weaverx triage --repo owner/repo --issue 1234 --dry-run

# Post comment (requires token + explicit confirm flags)
export GITHUB_TOKEN=ghp_...
weaverx triage --repo owner/repo --issue 1234 --post-comment --confirm

More examples: llm_provider_examples.md.

docs.title: Installation
docs.text: WeaveRx is published on PyPI as weaverx (lowercase on PyPI; the GitHub repo is titled WeaveRx).

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text: • Python 3.11+ (3.11 and 3.12 tested in CI)

docs.title: Recommended: use a virtual environment
docs.text:
Install into an isolated environment so dependencies do not conflict with system Python:

python -m venv .venv

# Linux / macOS
source .venv/bin/activate

# Windows (PowerShell)
.venv\Scripts\Activate.ps1

Then install WeaveRx inside the active environment.

docs.title: Install from PyPI
docs.text:
pip install weaverx

Verify:

weaverx --help

docs.title: Install from GitHub
docs.text:
Pin a release tag if you need a specific version:

pip install git+https://github.com/FratresMedAI/WeaveRx.git@v0.1.3

docs.title: Development install
docs.text:
For local changes or contributions:

git clone https://github.com/FratresMedAI/WeaveRx.git
cd WeaveRx
python -m venv .venv
source .venv/bin/activate   # or .venv\Scripts\Activate.ps1 on Windows
pip install -e ".[dev]"
pre-commit install

Run the offline test suite:

pytest -m "not network" --cov=weaverx

See Contributing for the full workflow.

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text:
weaverx: command not found after install

  • Ensure your virtual environment is activated, or use python -m weaverx --help.
  • On some systems, add ~/.local/bin to your PATH when installing with pip install --user.

No matching distribution found for weaverx

  • Confirm Python 3.11+: python --version.
  • Upgrade pip: python -m pip install --upgrade pip.

Permission errors during install

  • Prefer a virtual environment instead of sudo pip install.
  • On managed machines, ask your admin or use pip install --user weaverx.

GitHub install fails

  • Ensure Git is installed and reachable.
  • Pin an existing tag (e.g. @v0.1.3) rather than a branch name.

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
Set API keys and defaults via environment variables — see Configuration.

Quick mock demo (no keys):

weaverx triage --repo Project-MONAI/MONAI --issue 42 --mock

docs.title: GitHub Action
docs.text:
Use a release tag in workflows:

- uses: FratresMedAI/WeaveRx@v0.1.3
  with:
    repo: ${{ github.repository }}
    issue_number: ${{ github.event.issue.number }}
    dry_run: "true"
  env:
    XAI_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.XAI_API_KEY }}

See action.yml on GitHub.

docs.title: Documentation site
docs.text: Browse the full docs at fratresmedai.github.io/WeaveRx.

docs.title: Releasing WeaveRx
docs.text:
Semantic version tags trigger the release workflow, which:

  1. Runs ruff, mypy, and pytest with coverage
  2. Builds sdist + wheel
  3. Publishes to PyPI (trusted publishing)
  4. Creates a GitHub Release with artifacts attached

Live docs deploy automatically on push to master via the docs workflow to fratresmedai.github.io/WeaveRx.

docs.title: One-time PyPI trusted publishing setup
docs.text:
Before the first v* tag publish succeeds on PyPI:

  1. Create the weaverx project on pypi.org (or claim the name if reserved).
  2. Add a trusted publisher:
  3. PyPI publisher name: pypi
  4. Repository: FratresMedAI/WeaveRx
  5. Workflow: release.yml
  6. Environment name: (leave blank)
  7. Push a tag — no PYPI_API_TOKEN secret required.

docs.title: Cut a release
docs.text:
1. Update CHANGELOG.md.
  2. Bump version in pyproject.toml and src/weaverx/__init__.py.
  3. Commit and push to master.
  4. Tag and push:
git tag -a v0.1.2 -m "v0.1.2"
git push origin v0.1.2

docs.title: Verify locally before tagging
docs.text:
pip install -e ".[dev]"
ruff check .
mypy src/weaverx
pytest --cov=weaverx --cov-fail-under=75
python -m build
mkdocs build --strict

docs.title: Install after release
docs.text:
pip install weaverx

Fallback:

pip install git+https://github.com/FratresMedAI/WeaveRx.git@v0.1.2

docs.title: Documentation setup
docs.text:
Local preview:

pip install -e ".[docs]"
mkdocs serve

Open http://127.0.0.1:8000

Build (strict):

mkdocs build --strict

Screenshots are generated separately:

pip install -e ".[dev]"
pip install playwright
playwright install chromium
python scripts/generate_doc_screenshots.py

See screenshots/README.md for asset details.

docs.title: Medical AI categories
docs.text: Category What we look for Dataset Access & Licensing Download links, usage terms, attribution Model Performance (Pathology/Subgroup) Accuracy on specific diseases or patient groups Reproducibility & Environment MONAI/nnU-Net versions, CUDA/PyTorch, can't reproduce results Clinical Validation Request External validation, reader studies, deployment Privacy/Compliance/DICOM PHI, de-identification, HIPAA/GDPR, DICOM metadata Bug Crashes, incorrect outputs Feature/Integration Request New capabilities, framework hooks Documentation Missing or unclear tutorials and API docs

docs.title: CLI reference
docs.text:
weaverx triage --repo owner/repo [--issue N | --recent N] [options]
Flag Description --mock Offline mode (sample GitHub + mock LLM) --mock-llm Real GitHub, offline mock LLM --dry-run Analyze only; never write (default unless posting) --json Machine-readable JSON output --llm-provider grok, anthropic, or openai --confirm Required to post comments or apply labels --post-comment Post draft (needs --confirm) --apply-labels Apply suggested labels (needs --confirm) --privacy-insight Flag possible PHI/DICOM concerns (default: on) --safeguards / --no-safeguards Local draft heuristics (default: on) -v, --verbose Full draft, sources, debug logging

docs.title: JSON output fields
docs.text:
Field Description status ready_for_review or posted issue GitHub issue metadata analysis Raw LLM triage (category, priority, labels, raw draft, reasoning) sources Issue excerpts that grounded the triage draft_response Refined postable comment (use this for posting) safeguard Local heuristic score, status, triggered flags, metrics llm Provider and model used duplicate_matches Similar recent issues (heuristic, not embeddings yet)

Full sample: sample_triage_output.json.

docs.title: LLM providers
docs.text:
Provider CLI API key env Default model Grok --llm-provider grok XAI_API_KEY xai/grok-2-latest Anthropic --llm-provider anthropic ANTHROPIC_API_KEY anthropic/claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022 OpenAI-compatible --llm-provider openai OPENAI_API_KEY openai/gpt-4o

Override model globally: WEAVERX_LLM_MODEL=anthropic/claude-3-5-haiku-20241022

Override provider default: WEAVERX_LLM_PROVIDER=anthropic

All providers return the same structured JSON schema (TriageAnalysis + sources).

Copy-paste commands: llm_provider_examples.md.

Environment variables: Configuration.

docs.title: Draft safeguards
docs.text:
After generating a draft, WeaveRx runs fast, local-only checks — no LLM calls on the safeguard path. Flags are advisory; they never auto-block posting.

Check Threshold (default) What it detects Entropy Shannon > 5.5 bits/char Possible encoded or obfuscated content Length > 6000 characters Suspiciously long drafts Repetition ratio > 0.35 or repeated 4-grams Heavy copy-paste or looped text Patterns regex heuristics Credential-like strings, base64 blobs, private key markers, excessive markdown links (>15) Relevance keyword overlap < 0.08 Draft may be off-topic vs issue Score Status Meaning 0.0 – 2.9 clean No meaningful red flags 3.0 – 6.9 review_recommended Skim draft before posting 7.0 – 10.0 high_risk Multiple or severe heuristics fired

Disable: --no-safeguards or WEAVERX_SAFEGUARDS=0. Tune: WEAVERX_SAFEGUARD_ENTROPY_MAX, WEAVERX_SAFEGUARD_MAX_CHARS.

See the safeguard warning screenshot and sample_safeguard_warning.json.

Complements privacy keyword scanning. Safire is a separate path for deeper audit tooling.

docs.title: V0.1.2
docs.text:
WeaveRx v0.1.2 is the launch polish release.

PyPI is now live — pip install weaverx

This release focuses on making WeaveRx easier to adopt for medical AI maintainers:

  • Dedicated installation guide with virtual environment recommendations and troubleshooting
  • Cleaner README with improved badge layout and a short, pointer-first Installation section
  • Professional shields.io badges and better first-impression structure
  • Strengthened safety defaults and responsible use messaging throughout
  • GitHub Action install path fixed so uses: FratresMedAI/WeaveRx@v* works cleanly from external repos
  • Full governance docs (ETHICS.md, SECURITY.md, etc.) and MkDocs site now live

Quick start

pip install weaverx
weaverx triage --repo Project-MONAI/MONAI --issue 42 --mock

See the full documentation and CHANGELOG.

Roadmap items (embedding duplicate detection, PR triage mode, expanded medical-AI presets) remain planned for future releases.

Thanks to everyone who gave early feedback.

Full Changelog: https://github.com/FratresMedAI/WeaveRx/compare/v0.1.1...v0.1.2

docs.title: V0.1.3
docs.text:
WeaveRx v0.1.3 is the public launch release — polished docs, hardened core paths, and PyPI-ready install.

PyPI: pip install weaverx

docs.title: Highlights
docs.text:
• Launch-ready README — compact hero, centered badges (PyPI, Python, MIT, CI, Docs), pointer-first installation
  • Installation guide — fratresmedai.github.io/WeaveRx/installation/ with virtualenv setup and troubleshooting
  • Smarter triage drafts — few-shot LLM examples + category-specific refinement (repro, dataset access, clinical validation, privacy, duplicates)
  • Resilient GitHub client — retries on rate limits, typed errors (403/404/429), pagination for comments and recent issues
  • Safety unchanged — dry-run by default, --confirm for writes, local safeguard heuristics

docs.title: Quick start
docs.text:
pip install weaverx
weaverx triage --repo Project-MONAI/MONAI --issue 42 --mock

Docs: https://fratresmedai.github.io/WeaveRx/

docs.title: What's next
docs.text:
Embedding-based duplicate detection, PR triage mode, and expanded medical-AI prompt presets remain on the roadmap.

Thank you to early testers — issues and feedback welcome on GitHub.

Full Changelog: https://github.com/FratresMedAI/WeaveRx/compare/v0.1.2...v0.1.3

docs.title: Files

docs.title: Regenerate
docs.text:
pip install -e ".[dev]"
pip install cairosvg              # Linux/macOS
pip install playwright            # Windows fallback
playwright install chromium       # if using playwright
python scripts/generate_doc_screenshots.py

The script uses the same Rich renderer as the CLI (render_triage_result) at 100 columns on a dark terminal theme (Rich MONOKAI). The safeguard screenshot uses a synthetic draft that triggers local heuristics — no new CLI flags.

Text fallbacks (accessibility): examples/captures/.
